description: Ofsted is the Office for Standards in Education, Children’s Services and Skills. We inspect services providing education and skills for learners of all ages. We also inspect and regulate services that care for children and young people. Ofsted is a non-ministerial department.

details.body:
Ofsted is the Office for Standards in Education, Children’s Services and Skills. We inspect services providing education and skills for learners of all ages. We also inspect and regulate services that care for children and young people.

Ofsted is a non-ministerial department.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: This page explains Ofsted report cards for your child’s education or childcare, including a video and guidance on what’s changed from the old system.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Understanding Ofsted report cards and grades

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Ofsted and the Care Quality Commission (CQC) have today published guidance for the next series of area SEND thematic visits.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Ofsted will begin inspecting early education and childcare providers more frequently from April 2026, in line with the DfE’s Best Start in Life strategy.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Ofsted to inspect early years providers more frequently

social_media_links.title: Developments in social care inspection blog

details.body: His Majesty’s Chief Inspector (HMCI) is statutorily responsible for Ofsted’s inspection and regulatory work. He is able to draw on the full range of our inspection findings to report on the quality of education, children’s services and skills, locally and nationally. He is responsible to both the Ofsted Board and to Parliament for the organisation, staffing and management of Ofsted, and for ensuring the efficient and effective use of our resources.

role_appointments.title: Sir Martyn Oliver - His Majesty’s Chief Inspector

details.body:
The director leads the development of education policy, guidance and training for inspections in:

  • early years
  • state-funded schools
  • further education and skills
  • non-association independent schools
  • unregistered schools
  • teacher development
  • area special educational needs and/or disabilities

role_appointments.title: Lee Owston - National Director, Education

details.body:
The Director, Strategy and Engagement oversees the development and implementation of Ofsted’s strategic priorities. He is accountable to the Ofsted Board for the delivery of strategic objectives.

The Director, Strategy and Engagement also leads on Ofsted’s engagement with senior stakeholders, cross-government relationships, academics and policy makers to make sure the way Ofsted works is informed by their evidence.

The Director manages Ofsted’s communications and legal functions, ensuring that Ofsted is transparent and accountable, and works within the powers given to it by Parliament.

role_appointments.title: Rory Gribbell - Director, Strategy and Engagement

details.body: The Ofsted Academy is where all of Ofsted’s work to attract, retain, train and develop inspectors and administrative, professional and technical (APT) colleagues comes together. The Academy seeks to open Ofsted up to those we inspect and regulate, and pass on what we know. It seeks to invest in our people, enabling all Ofsted staff to engage in consistency and improvement, and continue to build Ofsted’s culture of professionalism, empathy, courtesy and respect.

role_appointments.title: Matthew Purves - Director, Ofsted Academy

details.body: The National Director, Digital and Operations is responsible for leading and managing the delivery of Ofsted’s digital and technology services to make sure they meet the needs of internal and external users. They are also responsible for Ofsted’s operational support services.

role_appointments.title: Neil Greenwood - Director, Digital and Information

details.body: The Chief Financial Officer (CFO) provides strategic financial leadership within Ofsted and in its interactions with the rest of the government. The CFO oversees Ofsted’s budget and serves as His Majesty’s Chief Inspector’s key adviser on public finances, taking responsibility for all aspects of finance. This includes leading financial planning, reporting, and compliance to ensure value for money and effective resource use. Additionally, the CFO manages property and commercial services within Ofsted.

role_appointments.title: Louise Grainger - Chief Financial Officer

details.body: The Director, Finance, Planning and Commercial serves as the Finance Director for Ofsted. They lead Ofsted’s strategic financial planning and management and support the Chief Operating Officer (COO).

role_appointments.title: Louise Grainger - Director, Finance, Planning and Commercial

details.body: The National Director, Delivery is responsible for inspection performance across all of Ofsted’s inspection remits including early years, schools, further education and skills, social care and teacher training. Additionally, the National Director, Delivery oversees the work of the Risk Assessment, Selection and Forecasting (RASAF) team and the unregistered schools team.

role_appointments.title: Andrew Cook - National Director, Delivery

details.body: The Ambassador represents His Majesty The King and the UK government in the country to which they are appointed. They are responsible for the direction and work of the embassy and its consulates, including political work, trade and investment, press and cultural relations and consular services.

details.body: Ofsted’s Director, Insights and Research leads the research, analysis and statistics functions, ensuring that everything Ofsted does is grounded in evidence. The role is responsible for ensuring that the required data and information are available to support Ofsted’s inspection and regulatory activities, and that insights gathered are used to support and influence Ofsted’s work and the sectors inspected and regulated.

details.description: Please contact your local council or call local police on 101 if you're worried about a child who is at risk of being harmed. If the child is in immediate danger, call police on 999. Our helpline is open from 9am to 5pm (Monday to Friday). Are you a provider? If so, please use the online notification forms below.

details.body:
The director leads the development of education policy, guidance and training for inspections in:

  • early years
  • state-funded schools
  • further education and skills
  • non-association independent schools
  • unregistered schools
  • teacher development
  • area special educational needs and/or disabilities

His Majesty’s Chief Inspector (HMCI) is statutorily responsible for Ofsted’s inspection and regulatory work. He is able to draw on the full range of our inspection findings to report on the quality of education, children’s services and skills, locally and nationally. He is responsible to both the Ofsted Board and to Parliament for the organisation, staffing and management of Ofsted, and for ensuring the efficient and effective use of our resources.

The Director, Strategy and Engagement oversees the development and implementation of Ofsted’s strategic priorities. He is accountable to the Ofsted Board for the delivery of strategic objectives.

The Director, Strategy and Engagement also leads on Ofsted’s engagement with senior stakeholders, cross-government relationships, academics and policy makers to make sure the way Ofsted works is informed by their evidence.

The Director manages Ofsted’s communications and legal functions, ensuring that Ofsted is transparent and accountable, and works within the powers given to it by Parliament.
The Director of People and Operations is responsible for Ofsted’s human resources, training, internal communications and our financial, legal and operational support services.

The Director, Digital and Information is responsible for leading and managing the delivery of Ofsted’s digital, technology and information services to make sure they meet the needs of internal and external users.
The Director, Finance, Planning and Commercial serves as the Finance Director for Ofsted. They lead Ofsted’s strategic financial planning and management and support the Chief Operating Officer (COO).

The Ofsted Academy is where all of Ofsted’s work to attract, retain, train and develop inspectors and administrative, professional and technical (APT) colleagues comes together. The Academy seeks to open Ofsted up to those we inspect and regulate, and pass on what we know. It seeks to invest in our people, enabling all Ofsted staff to engage in consistency and improvement, and continue to build Ofsted’s culture of professionalism, empathy, courtesy and respect.

details.body: The Chief Financial Officer (CFO) provides strategic financial leadership within Ofsted and in its interactions with the rest of the government. The CFO oversees Ofsted’s budget and serves as His Majesty’s Chief Inspector’s key adviser on public finances, taking responsibility for all aspects of finance. This includes leading financial planning, reporting, and compliance to ensure value for money and effective resource use. Additionally, the CFO manages property and commercial services within Ofsted.

The National Director, Delivery is responsible for inspection performance across all of Ofsted’s inspection remits including early years, schools, further education and skills, social care and teacher training. Additionally, the National Director, Delivery oversees the work of the Risk Assessment, Selection and Forecasting (RASAF) team and the unregistered schools team.

Ofsted’s Director, Insights and Research leads the research, analysis and statistics functions, ensuring that everything Ofsted does is grounded in evidence. The role is responsible for ensuring that the required data and information are available to support Ofsted’s inspection and regulatory activities, and that insights gathered are used to support and influence Ofsted’s work and the sectors inspected and regulated.

The National Director, Digital and Operations is responsible for leading and managing the delivery of Ofsted’s digital and technology services to make sure they meet the needs of internal and external users. They are also responsible for Ofsted’s operational support services.
